questions.question: What is the purpose of default function arguments in C++?
questions.explanation: Default function arguments allow a function to have a default value for a parameter, which is used if no value is provided when the function is called.

questions.question: Can a default function argument be used in a function declaration?
questions.explanation: Yes, default function arguments can be used in a function declaration. The syntax is `func(param, param = default_value);`.

questions.question: What happens when a function is called with fewer arguments than it has parameters?
options: The function will be called with default arguments
The function will throw an error
questions.explanation: The function will be called with default arguments. If there are remaining parameters, they will be passed as default arguments.

questions.question: Can a function have multiple default function arguments?
questions.explanation: Yes, a function can have multiple default function arguments. They are specified in the order of their appearance in the function declaration.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `= default` syntax in default function arguments?
options: To specify the default value of a parameter
To specify the type of a parameter
To specify the name of a parameter
To specify the order of parameters
questions.explanation: To specify the default value of a parameter. This syntax is used to explicitly specify the default value of a parameter.
